name: lws-spec
description: JLWS — a personal clean-slate Linked Web Storage design: core protocol (wire-compatible superset of the LWS WG WD; RFC 9728 discovery, idempotent create, path alignment, CID-shaped storage description, SSE/WebSocket notifications, WebAuthn suite, optional negotiated PoP, strict-ODRL grants, normative SSRF/threat model) + the RDF Content Transformation opt-in profile + the executable access-decision semantics (semantics/access-decision.n3: the normative N3 rule set defining evaluate-access — permit-derivation over recorded grants, one-directional action inclusion, trailing-slash-guarded target coverage, conjunctive fail-closed constraints, deny = decision-time closed-world absence — run by EYE as the definitional oracle over every evaluate-access vector) + the conformance test-vector suite (test-vectors/: 154 language-neutral cases across 10 suites, agentic-solid-conformance format, spec-derived verdicts, GAPS.md — incl. the dpop-sk, webauthn, a2a-rdf-discovery, and rdf-transform-advertisement composition vectors) + the executable conformance runner (test-suite/: per-normative-statement verdicts keyed to the statement companions, E/A/P testability honoured, committed CSS-baseline scoreboard). Public (maintainer-authorised). docs/alignment/ records how the sibling specs (AC-SPARQL, DPoP-SK, a2a-rdf, WebAuthn re-auth, the Agentic Solid Note, agentic-solid-conformance) compose with JLWS.
tr: none (personal draft; W3C contribution only on the maintainer's decision)
